name: 7shifts
northStar: warm diner counter with neon chalk accents — a bright restaurant counter where indigo buttons, orange hand-drawn underlines, and lime highlighter marks sit on clean white and warm bone surfaces, with occasional black bands that make the colors glow.

dos: Use #4570ff exclusively for primary actions — pill button at 9999px radius, 12px × 24px padding, 16px weight 500 white text
Reach for #ff6808 (Ember Orange) for the signature personality accents: hero underlines, feature section backgrounds, outlined buttons — never for body text or neutral UI
Apply 20px radius to all cards, 9999px to all buttons and tags, 40px to hero plates and full-bleed section edges
Set display headings (64px and above) at -0.05em letter-spacing; tighten progressively to -0.02em at 24px and reach near-zero at 14px body
Pair the Nanum Pen Script only with hero or section-level headings, and only beneath a single keyword — it is punctuation, not a running style
Alternate full-bleed black bands with warm bone (#f1f0ec) sections to create the contrast rhythm that makes the pastel and lime accents feel alive
Use the four pastel card variants (#ebdcff, #d6e0ff, #c293f1, #193f78, #244f47) in rotation across feature grids to add color variety without introducing new tokens
donts: Don't fill buttons with #ff6808 — orange is an accent, not an action; the only filled action color is #4570ff
Don't apply Nanum Pen Script to body copy, navigation, buttons, or anything below heading level — it loses its personality when overused
Don't use shadows or heavy elevation to separate cards — the system relies on fill contrast (white on bone, pastel on white, black on photo), not drop shadows
Don't introduce new saturated colors for UI — the palette is intentionally tight: one indigo, one orange, one lime, and four pastel variants
Don't set body text below 14px or above 18px — the readable range is narrow and the negative tracking is calibrated for it
Don't use #c6ff94 (Lime Highlighter) as a background outside of dark or photo sections — on white it reads as low-contrast and washed out
Don't apply +0.025em opened tracking to anything other than 14px weight 700 eyebrow labels — it is reserved for that specific all-caps label style

colors.name: Indigo Bolt
colors.role: Violet action color for filled buttons, selected navigation states, and focused conversion moments.

colors.name: Ember Orange
colors.role: Orange accent for outlined action borders, linked labels, and lightweight interactive emphasis

colors.name: Lime Highlighter
colors.role: Green text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases.

colors.name: Lavender Mist
colors.role: Soft card surface, gentle section background — pastel variant for breaking up the white canvas without adding noise

colors.name: Ice Blue
colors.role: Cool pastel card surface, section wash — pairs with indigo to reinforce the primary brand hue at low intensity

colors.name: Lilac Bloom
colors.role: Saturated card background in feature card grid — bolder pastel, one of four colored card variants that rotate across feature rows

colors.name: Deep Navy
colors.role: Blue wash for highlight backgrounds, decorative bands, and soft emphasis behind content. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

colors.name: Forest Sage
colors.role: Muted teal card variant — grounded, earthy counterpoint in the four-color card rotation

colors.name: Warm Bone
colors.role: Secondary canvas, soft section backgrounds, neutral action borders — the off-white that keeps the page from feeling sterile

colors.name: Paper White
colors.role: Page background, card surfaces, button fills, body text on dark sections

colors.name: Ink Black
colors.role: Primary text, dark feature sections, filled dark comparison cards — creates the dramatic black bands that make the colors pop

colors.name: Hairline
colors.role: Borders, dividers, table rules, input borders — the workhorse neutral stroke

colors.name: Stone
colors.role: Mid-gray strokes, subtle dividers, muted icon backgrounds

colors.name: Smoke
colors.role: Secondary body text, helper text, muted captions

colors.name: Graphite
colors.role: Tertiary text, button labels on neutral buttons

designSystem.layout: Page is centered with a 1200px max-width content well on a pure white canvas. The hero is a centered text block — large 48–64px weight 500 headline, orange Nanum Pen Script underline accent, single indigo pill CTA — with no hero image. The page alternates between four content shapes: (1) centered text + CTA blocks on white, (2) side-by-side comparison cards in a 2-column grid with a light bone card paired against a black card, (3) full-bleed dark photographic bands for social proof, and (4) tabbed feature sections with a colored panel + white text panel split. Feature grids use 3–4 column card layouts with rotating pastel card fills. Section gaps run 64–80px, with 20–24px card padding and 8–12px element gaps. Navigation is a top bar: logo left, 6 nav links center, indigo CTA + bone login pill right. The overall density is compact but with generous breathing room around the dark bands, which act as full-bleed dividers between otherwise quiet content sections.
designSystem.imagery: Imagery is sparse and purposeful. The only full-bleed photograph is a dark, warm-lit restaurant interior (wood tones, ambient glow) used as a single trust/case-study band — it grounds the brand in its restaurant context without dominating. Product screenshots appear inside dark-bordered device frames with the app UI composited over the photo, showing real software rather than illustrations. No illustrations, no 3D renders, no abstract graphics. Icons are minimal: simple filled circles for check/cross marks in comparison lists. The visual weight is overwhelmingly typographic and layout-driven, with color (not imagery) doing the decorative work.

similar.why: Same restaurant-industry SaaS playbook: white canvas, single punchy CTA color, warm pastel card variants, and generous pill-shaped buttons
Same tight negative tracking on geometric sans-serif headings and a single saturated action color against an otherwise near-monochrome interface
Same centered-hero-on-white pattern with a single pill CTA, alternating light/dark comparison sections, and rounded 20px cards
Same mix of quiet monochrome UI with bursts of color used as section backgrounds and a script/handwritten accent for personality moments

surfaces.name: Warm Bone Section
surfaces.purpose: Secondary canvas, large content bands, soft section backgrounds

surfaces.name: Card Surface
surfaces.purpose: Product cards, content cards sitting on the bone background

surfaces.name: Dark Band
surfaces.purpose: Full-bleed dark sections, dramatic comparison cards, photo overlays

surfaces.name: Pastel Wash
surfaces.purpose: Soft tinted card and section variants for visual rhythm

designSystem.northStar: warm diner counter with neon chalk accents — a bright restaurant counter where indigo buttons, orange hand-drawn underlines, and lime highlighter marks sit on clean white and warm bone surfaces, with occasional black bands that make the colors glow.

components.name: Primary Pill Button
components.role: Main call-to-action — 'Get your free trial', demo requests
components.description: Filled #4570ff indigo, white text, 9999px radius (true pill), 12px vertical × 24px horizontal padding, weight 500 at 16px. Single shadow or none. This is the only filled chromatic button in the system.

components.name: Outlined Orange Button
components.role: Secondary action on hero or feature sections
components.description: Transparent fill, 2px #ff6808 border, #ff6808 text, 9999px radius, 10px × 20px padding. Reads as energetic, not aggressive.

components.name: Ghost Nav Button
components.description: Transparent fill, #555555 text at 16px weight 500, 8px vertical × 12px horizontal padding, 16px radius. Becomes filled #f1f0ec on hover.

components.name: Login Pill Button
components.role: Low-emphasis utility action in the header
components.description: Filled #f1f0ec warm bone, #000000 text, 9999px radius, 10px × 20px padding. Sits next to the primary indigo CTA to create a quiet/active pair.

components.name: Content Card
components.role: Feature cards, testimonial cards, product modules
components.description: White surface, 20px radius, 20–24px padding, optional 1px #e5e7eb hairline border. No shadow by default — the dark band context or colored fill provides the elevation.

components.name: Colored Feature Card
components.role: Rotating pastel/navy/forest card variant in feature grids
components.description: One of four fills — #ebdcff lavender, #d6e0ff ice blue, #c293f1 lilac, #193f78 navy, or #244f47 sage. 20px radius, 20–24px padding, white or black text depending on fill luminance. This rotation is a signature device for adding variety without breaking the system.

components.name: Dark Comparison Card
components.description: Filled #000000, white text, 20px radius, 24px padding. Paired against a light bone card with the same radius to create the side-by-side contrast block.

components.name: Hero Plate
components.role: Full-width hero container and case-study band
components.description: Full-bleed white or dark photo, 40px radius on the top or bottom edge when transitioning into a card grid. Contains centered headline at 48–64px weight 500 with the Nanum Pen Script orange underline accent.

components.name: Tabbed Feature Section
components.role: Product feature showcase with switchable tabs
components.description: White background, centered heading at 36–48px, tab row with active tab marked by an indigo underline and weight 500. Content below splits into a colored feature panel (orange or pastel) on the left and white text panel on the right, both at 20px radius.

components.name: Pill Tag / Case Study Chip
components.role: Case study links, category tags, small badges
components.description: Filled #ffffff or #f1f0ec, #000000 text at 12–14px weight 400, 9999px radius, 4px × 12px padding. Sits beneath logo grids.

components.name: Logo Grid
components.role: Customer trust strip — restaurant chain logos
components.description: Horizontal row of brand logos on a dark photo background, each with a white pill 'Case study' chip beneath. No card containers; logos sit directly on the photographic surface.

components.name: Comparison Check / Cross List
components.role: Bullet-style benefits list inside comparison cards
components.description: Each item is a 16px weight 500 label preceded by a circular checkmark (#000000 filled, white check) or cross (#000000 with white ×). Items stack at 8px row gap inside the card padding.

components.name: Hand-Drawn Underline Accent
components.role: Personality flourish under hero or section keywords
components.description: Nanum Pen Script at 28–30px in #ff6808, positioned beneath a single key word in the headline. Never used for more than one word per heading, and only on hero/feature sections — not on body copy.

typography.role: Regular — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

All UI, body, navigation, and headings. Weight 500 carries buttons, nav links, and sub-headings; weight 400 owns body copy; weight 700 reserved for eyebrow labels with opened tracking. The full range from 11px captions to 150px display headings makes this a single-family system doing everything.
typography.family: A geometric grotesque sans-serif (custom-marketed as the site font — Inter, Manrope, or DM Sans are close substitutes)
typography.lineHeight: 0.75–1.56 depending on size; tightest at display sizes (0.75–0.93), standard at body (1.4–1.5)
typography.substitute: Inter (closest match for the geometric forms and tight tracking), Manrope, DM Sans
typography.letterSpacing: Tight negative tracking throughout: -0.05em at 120–150px display, -0.03em at 48–64px headings, -0.02em to -0.025em at 24–40px sub-headings, near-zero at 14–18px body. The 700-weight eyebrow caps at 14px use +0.025em (opened tracking) to read as a label, not body.

typography.role: Hand-drawn personality accent — appears only under hero copy (the orange 'actually work' underline) and similar signature moments. Never used for body, UI, or navigation. This is the brand's most distinctive typographic move: a script that says 'we're human, not enterprise software'.
Medium — detected in extracted data but not described by AI
Bold — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

designSystem.description: 7shifts is a light, air-kissed SaaS canvas with a single punchy indigo as the call-to-action and a small cast of warm accents doing the personality work. The page reads like a clean restaurant counter: white surface, warm bone secondary panels, soft pastel card variants, and the occasional full-bleed black band that makes everything around it feel brighter. Orange is the signature flourish — it underlines hero words in a hand-drawn stroke, floods feature backgrounds, and marks tabbed content — while a lime-green acts as highlighter text inside dark sections. Components are generous and rounded (pill buttons, 20px cards, 40px hero plates) with a compact vertical rhythm. The lone script typeface, Nanum Pen Script, shows up only as a personality accent under hero copy and never on body or UI controls.

system"}],"datePublished":"2026-04-30T00:44:25.717Z","dateModified":"2026-04-30T00:44:25.717Z"}]}19:T81b,Quick Color Reference:
- primary action: #4570ff (filled action)
- Accent / personality: #ff6808 (Ember Orange)
- Highlight on dark: #c6ff94 (Lime)
- Text primary: #000000
- Text secondary: #6e6d6c (Smoke)
- Surface base: #ffffff (Paper White)
- Surface secondary: #f1f0ec (Warm Bone)
- Border: #e5e7eb (Hairline)

Example Component Prompts:

1. Create a hero section on white: centered 64px weight 500 headline in #000000 with the word 'actually' (or one key word) underlined in #ff6808 using Nanum Pen Script at 28px. Subhead at 18px weight 400 in #6e6d6c. Single indigo pill CTA at 9999px radius, #4570ff fill, white 16px weight 500 text, 12px × 24px padding. Section vertical padding 80px.

2. Create a before/after comparison: two cards side by side at 20px radius. Left card #f1f0ec fill, 24px padding, 36px weight 500 heading in #000000, three list items with 8px row gap, each prefixed by a filled black circle with a white × mark. Right card identical structure but #000000 fill, white text, and filled black circle with white check mark.

3. Create a feature grid of 4 cards in a row: each card 20px radius, 24px padding, varying pastel fills — #ebdcff, #d6e0ff, #c293f1, #193f78. Heading at 24px weight 500, body at 14px weight 400. Text color switches to white on #193f78 and #244f47, stays #000000 on the lighter pastels.

4. Create a tabbed product feature: white background section, centered 36px weight 500 heading. Tab row below with 5 tab labels in 16px weight 400 #6e6d6c; active tab is 16px weight 500 #000000 with a 2px #4570ff underline. Content below: left half is a #ff6808 orange panel (20px radius, 20px padding) containing a product screenshot frame; right half is a white panel (20px radius, 20px padding) with a 24px weight 500 heading and 14px body text.

5. Create a case study logo band: full-bleed dark restaurant photo background, 40px top radius. Row of 6 restaurant logos in white, evenly spaced. Beneath each logo, a white pill tag (9999px radius, #ffffff fill, 12px weight 400 #000000 text, 4px × 12px padding) reading 'Case study'.

designSystem.elevationPhilosophy: The system intentionally avoids drop shadows. Elevation is communicated through fill contrast — white cards on warm bone surfaces, pastel cards on white, black cards on photo backgrounds — and through the dramatic black bands that act as full-bleed separators. The absence of shadows is a deliberate choice that keeps the page feeling flat, modern, and graphic, letting the orange and lime accents do the visual lifting instead of shadow stacks.
